Noncardiogenic pulmonary edema during intrabiliary infusion of mono-octanoin.
We report a case of a 60-year-old woman affected by squamous lung carcinoma, who developed paroxysmal supraventricular tachycardia during cisplatin and etoposide combination chemotherapy.
A case is reported of theophylline intoxication due to a dramatic decrease in theophylline clearance following concomitant administration of viloxazine.
Theophylline intoxication following viloxazine induced decrease in clearance.
Acute myocardial necrosis during administration of amsacrine.
In addition to the recognized arrhythmic complications, the authors emphasize myocardial necrosis as a possible further manifestation of amsacrine-related cardiotoxicity.
The clinical findings and laboratory studies suggested an autoimmune cell-mediated hypersensitivity reaction triggered by phenobarbital.
Duodenal ulceration: a complication of tolazoline therapy.

The latter form (Macrodantin) is reported to engender less gastrointestinal intolerance but it can produce the same adverse effects as the conventional form--liver damage, acute and chronic pulmonary reactions, peripheral neuropathy, blood dyscrasias and allergic reactions--and does so just as rapidly and floridly; one such case is reported here.
Generalized pustular psoriasis precipitated by trazodone in the treatment of depression.
We report a patient in whom the anti-depressant trazodone hydrochloride (Molipaxin, Roussel), a serotonin antagonist, provoked generalized pustular psoriasis (GPP).

Intravenous verapamil therapy in babies may cause apnea, hypotension, and bradycardia; continued episodes of atrial flutter in a child may cause sudden death; quinidine may be related to the death; children with "familial seizure disorders" may in fact have the long QT interval syndrome.
Methimazole-associated cholestatic liver injury: case report and brief literature review.
The authors report a further case of methimazole-associated liver damage and present a brief review of eleven previous cases found in the literature.

The main clinical features of this 58-year-old female patient were laboratory evidence of leucopenia and cholestasis, and biopsy features of fatty liver parenchyma degeneration with granulocytic portal infiltration and bile stasis, demonstrated 20 days after the initiation of antithyroid therapy with 20 mg methimazole daily.
We report a case of penile fibrosis after intracavernous self-injection of a combination of phentolamine and papaverine.
A 57-year-old woman developed pulmonary sarcoidosis during therapy with interferon beta for advanced renal cell carcinoma metastatic to mediastinal lymph nodes.
The possible role of interferon beta in the pathogenesis of sarcoidosis in this patient is discussed.
Fatal outcome of intravesical formalin instillation, with changes mimicking renal tuberculosis.

We present a case report of a patient with alveolar hydatid disease, whose treatment with mebendazole was brief due to side effects of alopecia and granulocytopenia; and review the features of this disease which, with increasing international migration, may be encountered more frequently in Australia.
Antibiotic-associated colitis (pseudomembranous colitis) developed in four patients with spinal cord injury and taking oral trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole.
The condition should be suspected in patients who develop unexplained abdominal pain or collapse following vasopressin treatment.
Protamine-induced fatal anaphylaxis.
Ototoxicity as a result of cis-platinum administration is well documented.
Paradoxical pulmonary vasoconstriction induced by nitroglycerin in idiopathic pulmonary hypertension.
Ampicillin increased the preexisting electrical decrement in three rabbits with experimental autoimmune myasthenia gravis while the drug had no deleterious effects in less affected or normal animals.
Myasthenic patients receiving ampicillin should be closely monitored for possible acute exacerbations.

A study following large patient groups on theophylline and a combination of theophylline and steroids might clarify the risk of ulcer formation in patients being treated with these medications for asthma.
A case of propranolol overdose complicated by esophageal spasm preventing extrication of an orogastric lavage tube and relieved by intravenous glucagon is presented.
Esophageal spasm following propranolol overdose relieved by glucagon.
A patient presented with a painful, oedematous, cyanosed hand having injected a solution of diamorphine and methylphenidate into his radial artery.
Ischaemia following selfadministered intra-arterial injection of methylphenidate and diamorphine.
Cardiac decompensation following verapamil therapy in infants with supraventricular tachycardia.
The risk of cardiac decompensation in infants with supraventricular tachycardia and congestive cardiac failure should be kept in mind prior to administration of verapamil.
Pneumonitis with pleural and pericardial effusion and neuropathy during amiodarone therapy.
Heparin-induced hyperkalemia.

Our findings suggest that hyperkalemia can develop with the use of low-dose heparin, within seven days of initiating heparin therapy, and that patients with diabetes mellitus or chronic renal insufficiency are especially predisposed to this complication.
We present four cases in which the use of heparin was associated with hyperkalemia and discuss the pathophysiology.
While most physicians are aware of heparin-induced thrombocytopenia and skin necrosis, the association of heparin and hyperkalemia is less well recognized.

Careful follow-up of so-called "self-limited" insulin edema is encouraged, and the early institution of diuretic therapy is advocated in elderly patients to prevent the development of overt cardiac failure.
This report describes the first case of insulin-induced cardiac failure in a patient without underlying heart disease.
A case of pyridoxine-dependent seizures is reported.
Administration of pyridoxine to an infant after a long period of convulsions was followed by acute hypotonia.
In all the patients, pellagra symptoms appeared during isoniazid therapy.
Pellagra encephalopathy among tuberculous patients: its relation to isoniazid therapy.
Physicians should be aware of the potential for the development of RS among children who are receiving long-term aspirin therapy for the treatment of systemic inflammatory illnesses.
Reye syndrome (RS) is believed to occur infrequently among children receiving long-term aspirin therapy.
A 54-year-old man, treated with amiodarone, developed thyrotoxicosis.
Amiodarone-induced dysthyroidism.
IV propranolol was used as the initial treatment for his hyperadrenergic state, resulting in a decrease in heart rate but a paroxsymal increase in blood pressure.
Propranolol-induced hypertension in treatment of cocaine intoxication.
According to the literature, chlorambucil central nervous toxicity is found almost exclusively in childhood nephrotic syndrome.
EEG abnormalities were observed in two of the nine children during chlorambucil therapy.
A 35-year-old man with seropositive rheumatoid arthritis developed selective IgA deficiency during treatment with fenclofenac.
Fenclofenac-induced selective IgA deficiency in rheumatoid arthritis.

A 65-year-old woman with bipolar disorder and complicated cardiovascular disease who was on maintenance lithium therapy developed a movement disorder following high doses of trazodone for treatment of an acute depression.
Although the movement disorder could not with certainty be attributed to trazodone alone, the drug at least acted as an eliciting agent.
The elicitation of a movement disorder by trazodone: case report.
A review of the literature showed no previous description of this pattern in benzodiazepine coma.
Spindle coma in benzodiazepine toxicity: case report.
Two reports of spindle coma are noted with alcohol and imipramine.
A literature review revealed 83 other reported cases of rifampicin-induced renal insufficiency.
Rifampicin-induced renal failure.
The following is a report of 2 cases of rifampicin-induced renal insufficiency.
Cerebral and ocular toxicity induced by desferrioxamine.
Two of these patients, who also received the anti-emetic prochlorperazine, lost consciousness for 48-72 h and then fully recovered.
Three patients developed proteinuria following gold therapy for rheumatoid arthritis.
In all three cases, autopsy revealed gross and microscopic features indistinguishable from those seen in the Hamman-Rich syndrome, and methotrexate hepatotoxicity was present in one.
Methotrexate-induced diffuse interstitial pulmonary fibrosis.
Pulmonary eosinophilia or granulomas, classically seen in previously reported cases of methotrexate pneumonitis, were not observed.
Inappropriate antidiuretic hormone following adenine arabinoside administration.
The toxic effects of methotrexate included elevated liver transaminases (3/4), nausea (2/4), abdominal pain (2/4), bone pain (2/4), mild neutropenia (1/4), and mild pruritus (1/4).
Dystonia associated with carbamazepine administration: experience in brain-damaged children.
The currently available clinical and neuropharmacologic data suggest that carbamazepine may be an antagonist of dopamine and that this property is responsible for the production of dystonia.
We have cared for three children in whom four episodes of dystonia proceeding to opisthotonus occurred in association with carbamazepine use.
Aplastic anemia and agranulocytosis in patients using methazolamide for glaucoma.
Several case reports of aplastic anemia with use of acetazolamide, and two cases with use of methazolamide, have appeared in the literature.

This article describes two cases of aplastic anemia, at least one of which was almost certainly induced by the use of methazolamide, and one case of agranulocytosis related to the use of methazolamide.
Squamous-cell carcinoma arising in a basal-cell epithelioma treated with 5-fluorouracil.
The patient whose case is reviewed had migraine headaches and received methysergide maleate for 13 years.
A case of Erythema Multiforme Bullosum in patient of lepromatous leprosy with pulmonary tuberculosis due to Rifampicin is described.
Erythema multiforme bullosum due to rifampicin.
Renal damage associated with long term use of lithium carbonate.
The authors report 2 cases of renal damage associated with lithium carbonate treatment.
In two patients with mycosis fungoides, a squamous cell carcinoma developed during therapy with psoralens plus long-wave ultraviolet radiation (PUVA).
Hyperglycemia and diabetic coma: possible relationship to diuretic-propranolol therapy.
An insulin-dependent diabetic patient with nephropathy developed severe acidosis after treatment with acetazolamide for glaucoma.
Severe acidosis from acetazolamide in a diabetic patient.
Five cases of clindamycin-associated pseudomembranous colitis in leukaemic patients are reported.
An infertile patient with amenorrhea-galactorrhea syndrome lost vision during a pregnancy occurring after Bromocryptine treatment.
Fatal acute tubular necrosis occurred in 1 patient in whom intravesical formalin was used to control massive persistent hemorrhage from radiation cystitis.
Fatal complication of intravesical formalin during control of intractable hemorrhage from radiation cystitis.
A young woman developed galactorrhea during treatment with a new dibenzoxazepine antidepressant, amoxapine.
A 15-kg weight gain developed in a patient during the third week of ibuprofen therapy.
Edema associated with ibuprofen therapy.
It should be recognized that ibuprofen may be associated with salt and water retention in the same fashion as previously described with phenylbutazone and indomethacin.
Allopurinol-associated hand and foot deformities in chronic tophaceous gout.
Neurotoxicity of intrathecal administration of amphotericin B.

Cardiac glycosides are thought to prevent doxorubicin cardiomyopathy by competitively inhibiting doxorubicin at its receptor sites, but ouabain has a much shorter half life than doxorubicin and its metabolites and so is less effective than digoxin.
Doxorubicin cardiotoxicity: possible role of digoxin in its prevention.

Minor electrocardiographical changes were noted in five out of six patients who were not receiving a cardiac glycoside and four out of six who were receiving ouabain, and none of the 16 who were receiving digoxin.
One other patient on digoxin stopped taking it and developed cardiomyopathy.
One patient on ouabain also developed cardiomyopathy.
A healthy, 30-year-old man, exposed to sulindac on two separate occasions, had an incapacitating isolated idential sensory neuropathy.
Sulindac (Clinoril), a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory agent, has few reported neurologic toxic effects, all of which have been associated with systemic disease.
To our knowledge, we describe the first reported case of isolated paresthesia and peripheral neuropathy, without systemic involvement, secondary to sulindac administration.
In addition, loading with one low oral dose of serine produced psychotic symptoms 5 h later which lasted 3-6 h.
Reversal of ergotamine-induced arteriospasm by mechanical intra-arterial dilatation.
The authors describe a case of combined lithium and haloperidol toxicity characterized by hyperpyrexia, severe rigidity, mutism, and development of irreversible tardive dyskinesia.

The authors postulate that two types of combined lithium-neuroleptic toxicity occur: a neuroleptic malignant extrapyramidal syndrome and a lithium toxicity that occurs in combination with phenothiazines, primarily thioridazine.
Chlorambucil-induced chromosome damage to human lymphocytes is dose-dependent and cumulative.
Anaphylactoid reaction to 50% solution of dextrose.
Two patients with extrinsic asthma and coexistent insulin-dependent diabetes mellitus sustained an anaphylactoid reaction after the intravenous administration of 50% solution of dextrose.
Salicylate hepato toxicity in rheumatic fever.
Two children with rheumatic fever developed anicteric hepatitis while on high-dose aspirin therapy.

A 36-year-old man being treated with cisplatinum, vinblastine, and bleomycin for testicular carcinoma developed a dense left homonymous hemianopsia, encephalopathy, and a partial nondominant parietal lobe syndrome.
Transient left homonymous hemianopsia and encephalopathy following treatment of testicular carcinoma with cisplatinum, vinblastine, and bleomycin.
An association of granulocytopenia, eosinophilia, skin reaction and hepatitis during propylthiouracil (PTU) therapy for thyrotoxicosis in a 47 year old black female is reported.
Optic neuropathy associated with penicillamine therapy in a patient with rheumatoid arthritis.
Optic neuropathy developed in a patient with rheumatoid arthritis who had been receiving D-penicillamine for about 1 year.
The case resembles two previously reported cases of optic neuropathy which occurred in patients with Wilson's disease who were receiving penicillamine.
There was a clear relationship between restarting the Accutane and recurrence of the transient myopia.
Transient acute myopia resulting from isotretinoin (accutane) therapy.
Allergic contact dermatitis to compound tincture of benzoin.
Nineteen cases of allergic contact dermatitis to compound tincture of benzoin are described.
Autopsy evidence of herpesvirus infection was found in visceral organs of four leukemic patients who had received large doses of cytarabine (cytosine arabinoside; Ara-C) shortly before their death.
Visceral herpesvirus infections in leukemic patients receiving cytarabine.
Clinical and morphological features of gold neuropathy.
Three cases of gold-related neuropathy are reported.
Intra-neural ceroid-like pigment following the treatment of lepromatous leprosy with clofazimine (B663; Lamprene).
One of these was clofazimine, an aniline aposafranine derivative known to produce a ceroid-like pigment in the tissues of patients treated with this drug or lepromatous leprosy.
Severe neurotoxicity with methyl G: CALGB experience.
A young patient experienced marked sinus tachycardia while smoking marijuana and receiving therapeutic doses of nortriptyline.
It is concluded that 'cerebral atrophy' on CT scans may represent an accumulative effect of ACTH and that ACTH should be given with the utmost discretion, and in as low a dose as possible.
Disulfiram-like reactions with newer cephalosporins: cefmenoxime.
This is the first report of a disulfiram-like reaction with cefmenoxime.
Acute leukopenia associated with silver sulfadiazine therapy.
Patients from endemic areas referred to transplant centers may be at high risk for disseminated histoplasmosis when treated with long-term prednisone for graft-versus-host disease.
Acute renal failure most likely was secondary to the nephrotoxic effect of captopril on chronically hypoperfused kidneys.
In a patient with severe renovascular hypertension, nonoliguric acute renal failure developed after she received captopril treatment.
Nonoliguric acute renal failure after captopril therapy.
Captopril-induced acute reversible renal failure.
The 5 patients had severe renovascular disease which might thus represent a significant risk factor in the development of captopril-induced acute renal failure.
We report 5 cases of acute reversible renal failure coinciding with the onset of treatment with captopril in patients with severe drug-resistant hypertension.
A case of mania associated with fluoxetine.
A depressed woman with no history of bipolar illness developed a manic episode during treatment with fluoxetine.
Haemolytic-uraemic syndrome complicating long-term mitomycin C and 5-fluorouracil therapy for gastric carcinoma.
Three cases of acute renal toxicity in patients receiving long-term therapy with mitomycin C and 5-fluorouracil are reported.
Severe histological osteomalacia developed in a woman with Crohn's disease 2 years after ileal resection and the start of cholestyramine therapy.
Treatment of lithium tremor with metoprolol.
A 52-year-old Black woman on phenytoin therapy for post-traumatic epilepsy developed transient hemiparesis contralateral to the injury.
A possible mechanism for focal neurological deficit in brain-damaged patients on phenytoin therapy is discussed.
Transient hemiparesis caused by phenytoin toxicity.
A 7-year-old boy developed a severe unilateral grand mal seizure at the age of 5 years (phenobarbitone therapy); 1.5 years later valproate (2-propylpentanoic acid, VPA) was added to the therapy.
Cefoxitin therapy for Mycobacterium fortuitum bacteremia with associated granulomatous hepatitis.
Vasomotor reactions after gold sodium thiomalate are well recognized.
Gold nephropathy due to auranofin obscured by tolmetin pseudoproteinuria.
Cyclophosphamide can rarely cause interstitial pneumonitis and fibrosis.
Cyclophosphamide pneumonitis.

The temporal sequence of the respiratory insufficiency and the histopathology, when compared to the previous examples in the literature, suggest that cyclophosphamide was aetiologically responsible for the lung disease.
A reversible toxic liver damage was observed in a non-alcoholic woman treated with disulfiram.
Toxic hepatitis induced by disulfiram in a non-alcoholic.
Four patients receiving high-dose tamoxifen for greater than 1 year have demonstrated similar retinal changes.
Tamoxifen retinopathy.
These findings suggest that the corneal and retinal changes are the result of a toxic effect of tamoxifen when used in the doses and duration described.
Mammary gigantism is a rare complication of D-penicillamine treatment.
Ampicillin-associated seizures.
After the patient discontinued rifampicin, the skin lesions cleared completely within 5 weeks without any systemic medication.
Pemphigus foliaceus induced by rifampicin.
Pemphigus foliaceus was seen in a patient with pulmonary tuberculosis during rifampicin therapy.
In eight patients, a mean decrease in serum Na+ of 8.25 +/- 3.2 mEq/L was observed after a single 200 mg intravenous dose of lorcainide.
It is concluded that SIADH is an important side effect of lorcainide therapy.
One patient developed severe hyponatremia with serum Na+ of 108 mEq/L when hydrochlorothiazide was given to control hypertension.
Sixteen of 33 patients developed significant hyponatremia and hypoosmolality during oral treatment with lorcainide.
Chlorpropamide-induced hemolytic anemia.
In a 61-year-old man receiving chronic low-dosage amiodarone an interstitial pneumopathy was observed.
Alternating sinus rhythm and intermittent sinoatrial block induced by propranolol.
The abnormal rhythm disappeared with the withdrawal of propranolol and when the drug was restarted a 2/1 S-A block was seen.
This was accepted as evidence for propranolol being the cause of this conduction disorder.
A further case of anaphylactoid reaction to methylprednisolone is reported.
The responsibility of CCNU in the pathogenesis of pulmonary fibrosis seems very likely.
Two patients treated for chronic myeloid leukemia with high doses of CCNU (1100 mg/m2 and 1240 mg/m2, respectively) developed a fatal pulmonary fibrosis.
Additive pulmonary toxicity with melphalan and busulfan therapy.
We report a 59-year-old patient with chronic myeloid leukemia, who developed severe interstitial lung fibrosis after short term and sequential treatment with melphalan and busulfan.
After taking cholestyramine II sachets twice daily for two months she presented with lethargy, confusion and drowsiness.

The first reported case, in an adult, of cholestyramine induced hyperchloremic metabolic acidosis is a 70 year old female with a two year history of primary biliary cirrhosis confirmed by histologic and immunologic criteria.
Although myelosuppression is mild, immunosuppression and superinfection are potential hazards of treatment with DCF.
The ocular toxicity of DCF, previously described as conjunctivitis, appears to be a keratitis of moderate severity which requires further study.

Because there were no other obvious causes of renal failure in either patient, we propose that skeletal muscle ischemia developed during vasopressin infusion, followed by release of myoglobin and renal damage.
A major limitation in the use of amphotericin B is its potential to cause nephrotoxicity.
Amphotericin B nephrotoxicity in humans decreased by salt repletion.
Experience with five patients in whom impaired renal function developed early during amphotericin B therapy is reported.
Gynecomastia developed in two epileptic patients some months after the addition of oral fluoresone 750 mg daily to the phenobarbital and phenytoin already being administered.
Gynecomastia in epileptics treated with phenobarbital, phenytoin and fluoresone: two case reports.
Case report of withdrawal dyskinesia associated with amoxapine.
Occurrence of withdrawal dyskinesia indicates that the neuroleptic effects of amoxapine may be clinically significant.
A case of toxic hepatitis caused by combination therapy with methotrexate and etretinate in the treatment of severe psoriasis is presented in a 47-year-old woman.
Metabolic balance studies and rechallenge with hydrochlorothiazide were undertaken to investigate the mechanism of the thiazide-induced hyponatremia.
A 58-year-old woman developed unilateral acute angle-closure glaucoma four days after the application of a patch of transdermal scopolamine delivery system (TRANSDERM-V).
A case history of a 15-year-old boy who developed incapacitating tardive dyskinesia that resolved during treatment with deanol is presented.
Coagulopathy associated with the use of cephalosporin or moxalactam antibiotics in acute and chronic renal failure.
Moxalactam is more likely to be associated with platelet dysfunction.
Nevertheless, the coagulopathy seemed to be most closely associated with the administration of the cephalosporin.
A case is reported of a child with fatal pulmonary fibrosis following BCNU therapy.
Fatal pulmonary fibrosis following 1,3-bis(2-chloroethyl)-1-nitrosourea (BCNU) therapy.
To our knowledge, no prior cases of penicillamine-induced TTP in RA have been reported.
Amiodarone hydrochloride, a new antiarrhythmic agent, has been associated with pulmonary toxicity characterized by cough, dyspnea and diffuse pulmonary infiltrates.
Possible recurrence of amiodarone pulmonary toxicity following corticosteroid therapy.
To our knowledge, this recurrence of amiodarone pulmonary toxicity has not been reported previously.
We describe a case of fibrosing alveolitis, diagnosed by lung biopsy, in a patient receiving amiodarone which responded to corticosteroid therapy.
No other endogenous or exogenous cause for the gynecomastia could be found except for the theophylline.
The gynecomastia regressed when the theophylline was discontinued.
We report a case of sustained hypotension after administration of parenteral verapamil.
Drug-induced psychosis resulted from the administration of quinacrine hydrochloride at a dosage of 100 mg twice daily for the treatment of discoid lupus.
A case of priapism associated with trazodone is described.
Charcoal haemoperfusion and cysteamine therapy led to the rapid removal of the paracetamol from the body and consequently prevented the development of severe hepatic necrosis.
Anaphylactoid shock, disseminated intravascular coagulation, and anuric renal failure requiring dialysis occurred in a patient receiving zomepirac sodium for toothache.
Focal renal cortical necrosis associated with zomepirac.
Although the data indicate an immune-complex cause for gold-salt nephropathy, the incident antigen (or antigens) and mechanism of action remain unidentified.
Gold-salt therapy may result in damage to proximal tubules that leak renal tubular antigens, which in turn complex with autoantibody and produce an autoimmune membranous nephropathy.
The nephrotic syndrome developed in a patient receiving therapy with gold for rheumatoid arthritis.
Intrahepatic cholestasis and cutaneous bullae associated with glibenclamide therapy are described in a 61-year-old diabetic patient who presented wit hypoglycaemic coma.
In four patients, spasm occurred spontaneous and in one patient after 0.05 mg of ergonovine.
The authors describe a woman with chronic schizophrenia who experienced delirium, grand mal seizure, and photosensitivity after the addition of propranolol to her neuroleptic regimen.
Catatonia associated with disulfiram therapy.
Neurophysiological mechanisms which aid in elucidating the role of disulfiram in the etiology of catatonia are discussed.
This is the first report of a catatonic syndrome occurring in a patient receiving disulfiram treatment.
Hepatic reactions to cyclofenil.
Thirty patients with hepatic reactions to cyclofenil, a non-steroidal drug with a stimulating effect on ovulation, are reviewed.

Since early symptoms of the toxic effects of theophylline can mimic peptic ulcer disease, cimetidine might be prescribed for the gastrointestinal symptoms with subsequent worsening of theophylline poisoning.
Prior reports have emphasized the tubular and interstitial lesions associated with intermittent or discontinuous rifampin therapy for tuberculosis.
The spectrum of renal lesions occurring during antituberculous therapy, particularly in association with rifampin, may be wider than previously suspected.
Within 3 weeks of beginning continuous daily isoniazid and rifampin therapy for pulmonary tuberculosis, a patient developed acute renal failure.
Nephrotic range pseudoproteinuria in a tolmetin-treated patient.
When tuberculosis patients on isoniazid eat certain varieties of fish they may develop a histamine reaction.
A 45-year-old woman with thyrotoxicosis developed agranulocytosis after treatment with propylthiouracil.
The patient was given methimazole instead of propylthiouracil but, 10 weeks later, agranulocytosis again occurred.
This is, to the best of our knowledge, the first report of a case in which agranulocytosis followed treatment with both propylthiouracil and methimazole in the same patient.
Intrahepatic cholestasis and sicca complex after thiabendazole.
She was placed on adjuvant Adriamycin (doxorubicin) chemotherapy, but 6 months later died of Adriamycin toxicity.
Dural sinus thrombosis developed in two children with acute lymphoblastic leukemia during induction treatment with vincristine sulfate, prednisone, and asparaginase.
However, the amount of prednisone required to maintain normotension resulted in Cushingoid features and has been discontinued.
Clinicians should be aware of fasting hypoglycemia as an unusual but potentially serious complication of disopyramide therapy.
Cefoxitin-associated renal failure.
The other woman had rheumatoid arthritis and developed acute tubular necrosis after treatment with gentamicin and cefoxitin.
Two elderly women suffered an acute deterioration of renal function after treatment with cefoxitin sodium.
A 44-year-old woman is described in whom amiodarone, disopyramide, and quinidine, administered alone separately, induced atypical ventricular tachycardia (AVT, torsade de pointes).
Amiodarone was the third drug to induce AVT in this patient; she received 200 mg/day six days per week for six months.
Two years later, 24 hours following an increase in the dose of disopyramide from 300 to 600 mg/day, AVT with syncope occurred; isoproterenol abolished the arrhythmia instantly.

Although the existence of an acute pericarditis or an acute myocarditis as possible causes of the ST elevation cannot be fully ruled out, the sudden onset, prominent magnitude, and brief duration of the ST elevation are perhaps more indicative of an acute ischemic event, possibly related to a transient coronary vasoconstriction induced by the dopamine infusion.
Complete remission of the nephrosis occurred after discontinuation of hydroxychloroquine therapy.
The case of an adult who developed both hepatic dysfunction and an impaired macrophage migration after exposure to cimetidine is discussed.
There have been only two reports of cimetidine-induced hepatitis.

A 66-year-old mand suffering from severe coronary heart disease took digoxin with suicidal intent an was treated for the ensuing complete atrioventricular block with digoxin-specific antibody fragments.
Verapamil in effort-induced angina pectoris in patients with normal coronary arteries.
Flucytosine-associated diarrhea has been previously described in 6%-10% of patients receiving the drug.
This report describes a case of flucytosine-associated ulcerating enteritis in which the small bowel x-ray demonstrated severe luminal narrowing, ulceration, and marked separation of loops of bowel.

relations.effect: marked separation of loops of bowel

text: Ulcerating enteritis associated with flucytosine therapy.
Papilledema without peripheral neuropathy in a patient taking perhexiline maleate.
An adolescent male developed acute pancreatitis and pseudocyst of the pancreas 16 weeks after cessation of intramuscular L-asparaginase.
L-asparaginase-induced pancreatitis has been reported during or closely following administration of the drug.
Three cases of pseudocyst of the pancreas in two women and one man have previously been reported with the use of intravenous L-asparaginase.
Nine delirious patients suffering from lithium intoxication were examined with the Mini-Mental State Exam (MMS) to describe the clinical course of the disorder.
The course of delirium due to lithium intoxication.
Life-threatening hyperkalemia induced by arginine.
A 58-year-old man with rheumatoid arthritis developed lichen planus during treatment with gold.
Lichen planus and acne provoked by gold.

The case history confirms that gold treatment, even in the same patient, can give rise to a wide range of skin disturbances, which in many cases do not break out until long after the drug has been withdrawn.
Nephrotic syndrome associated with lithium therapy.
Two patients on long-term lithium therapy developed the nephrotic syndrome.
A 73-year-old woman with non-Hodgkin's lymphoma had two episodes of severe, bilateral, sensori-neural hearing loss after vincristine therapy.
Acute acoustic nerve palsy associated with vincristine therapy.
Bilateral acoustic (VIII) nerve palsy in this patient was most likely a manifestation of vincristine neurotoxicity.
Fatal ventricular fibrillation after treatment with digoxin in a 27-year-old man with mitral leaflet prolapse syndrome.
He was started on digoxin, 0.25 mg daily, because of echocardiographically demonstrated left ventricular dilatation and functional impairment; he died of ventricular fibrillation 15 days later.
Liver disease induced by perhexiline maleate.
Acute non-lymphocytic leukemia occurred in eight women following long-term treatment with Treosulfan (= dihydroxybusulfan) for ovarian carcinoma.
The probability of developing acute leukemia in this study was not significantly correlated to the total cumulative dosage of Treosulfan.
Propranolol-related bronchospasm in patients without history of asthma.
Since the bronchospasm was relieved with discontinuation of propranolol and supportive bronchodilator therapy, the bronchospasm was believed to be caused by propranolol.
The potential role of propranolol in inducing central nervous system disturbances is emphasized, and the literature on the subject is reviewed.
Interstitial fibrosis of the lung is a potential complication of methotrexate therapy for psoriasis.
Progressive interstitial lung disease from prolonged methotrexate therapy.
Abnormalities of the pupil and visual-evoked potential in quinine amblyopia.

Total blindness with a transient tonic pupillary response, denervation supersensitivity, and abnormal visual-evoked potentials developed in a 54-year-old man after the use of quinine sulfate for leg cramps.

relations.effect: Total blindness with a transient tonic pupillary response

text: A case of skeletal fluorosis induced by prolonged treatment with niflumic acid, a fast-acting non-steroid antiinflammatory agent, is reported in a 35-year-old woman suffering from rheumatoid arthritis and treated, in addition, with corticosteroids.

After ruling out a hydrotelluric source of fluorine, the patient's fluorosis was linked to chronic use of niflumic acid, following the publication in 1978 of the 2 previously reported cases affected by this drug.
Niflumic acid-induced skeletal fluorosis: iatrogenic disease or therapeutic perspective for osteoporosis?
The fluorine contained in niflumic acid induced a marked densification of trabecular bone in all 3 cases.
Hepatotoxicity associated with use of D-penicillamine in rheumatoid arthritis.
These cases and a review of the literature suggested that hepatotoxicity, though rare, should be added to the list of adverse reactions to D-penicillamine.
A 62-year-old Indian with diabetic nephropathy controlled with metformin, developed miliary tuberculosis for which he was treated with rifampicin, isoniazid and ethambutol.
Para-aminosalicylic acid-induced hypoglycaemia in a patient with diabetic nephropathy.
A patient developed papilloedema and hepatic dysfunction while being treated with perhexiline maleate.
Papilloedema and hepatic dysfunction apparently induced by perhexiline maleate (Pexid).
Markedly increased pigmementation of skin immediately overlying veins used for multiple 5-fluorouracil infusions was noted.
Unusual pigmentary changes associated with 5-fluorouracil therapy.
Although useful in the management of chronic alcoholism, disulfiram is being increasingly associated with a wide spectrum of side effects and untoward medical sequelae, which now include catatonia.
Disulfiram encephalopathy as a cause of the catatonia syndrome.
The authors describe a case of the catatonia syndrome associated with disulfiram therapy.
Inappropriate antidiuretic hormone secretion after high dose vinblastine.
We report on a patient with an embryonal teratocarcinoma of the testicle who had the syndrome of inappropriate secretion of antidiuretic hormone after receiving a high dose of vinblastine.
